title: HTML Link Extractor
shortDescription: Extract href values, URLs, anchor text and rel data from raw HTML in your browser.
metaTitle: HTML Link Extractor | Href extractor for HTML
metaDescription: Extract URLs from HTML, pull href values and review anchor text online. Helpful for searches like href extractor, extract links from HTML and extract URLs from HTML file.
intro: Use this HTML link extractor when you need an href extractor for audits, migrations or quick markup reviews. It is built for common search intent around extract URLs from HTML, extract links from HTML file and checking anchor text without running a separate crawler.
overview: Paste HTML and get a clean list of links with href, visible text, rel values and target attributes. The tool is useful for SEO reviews, content migrations, QA passes on templates and fast inspection of raw HTML copied from a page or file.
howItWorks: Paste the HTML snippet or source you want to inspect.
The extractor scans the markup for links and related attributes.
Review the output list and copy the link data you need for audits or documentation.
useCases: Extract href values from raw HTML quickly.
Review anchor text for SEO and content audits.
Check rel attributes like nofollow, sponsored and ugc.
Turn copied markup into a usable link inventory.

examples.title: Href extractor
examples.output: href: https://example.com | text: Visit site | rel: nofollow
examples.description: Useful when you need a fast href extractor for one or many links.

examples.title: Extract URLs from HTML file
examples.description: Helpful for pulling links out of copied template code or page source.

faq.question: Can this tool extract URLs from HTML files and snippets?
faq.answer: Yes. Paste the HTML source and the tool will list the URLs, anchor text and related attributes it finds.

faq.question: Is this the same as an href extractor?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed for the same job and also shows extra link context such as text, rel and target values.
